description: Generic, broadly-useful guards for the MAIN Claude Code session. Wire via .claude/settings.json (Bash matcher). Conservative on purpose: nudges help the model self-correct; only a couple of genuinely destructive, irreversible patterns block. Tune to your repo, then grow the set from your own telemetry with bin/derive_rules.py (see docs/TELEMETRY.md). Every rule records meta.why so you can prune it as models improve. Each rule carries an `examples` block (should_fire / should_not_fire) that bin/check_rules.py runs as a mini-spec.

rules.message: Refusing `rm -rf` targeting / , ~ or $HOME — this is an irreversible, catastrophic delete. Delete a specific project path instead, or narrow the target.

meta.why: irreversible filesystem loss; the one class worth a hard block by default.

rules.message: Piping a downloaded script straight into a shell (curl|sh) runs unreviewed remote code. Download to a file, inspect it, then run it — or install via a package manager with a pinned version.

meta.why: remote-code-execution footgun; recoverable, so nudge not block.

rules.message: Force-pushing a protected branch (main/master/prod/release) rewrites shared history and can drop teammates' commits. Use --force-with-lease, push a feature branch and open a PR, or confirm this is intentional.

meta.why: destructive to shared history but recoverable via reflog; nudge.

rules.message: A live-looking secret (cloud key / API token / inline password) appears in this command — it will land in shell history and any tool log. Read it from an env var or a secrets manager instead of pasting the literal, and rotate it if it was already exposed.

meta.why: secret leakage into history/logs; recoverable by rotating, so nudge.

rules.message: Bare `psql` with no connection target falls back to a local socket and usually fails (FATAL: database "<user>" does not exist). Pass a full postgresql:// URL, use -h/-d, or set PGHOST/PGDATABASE first.

meta.why: common recoverable failure; example of a telemetry-derived nudge.
